explanation: Uses PowerShell backtick (`) as a character escape/line continuation marker within the string. While PowerShell executes the command normally, a simple string match for 'Get-Clipboard' in the CommandLine will fail.
Uses string concatenation combined with the Invoke-Expression (IEX) cmdlet. The rule looks for the literal string 'Get-Clipboard', which is not present in the command line during process creation.
Uses the PowerShell .NET API to invoke the cmdlet. This avoids placing the target string directly into a standard command-line format that simple filters often look for.
Encodes the 'Get-Clipboard' command in Base64. The command line seen by the process creation log will contain the encoded string instead of the cleartext cmdlet name.
Defines a custom alias for the cmdlet within the session and then executes it. The command line only shows the alias creation and execution, not the direct call to 'Get-Clipboard'.
